item.title: Body painted myself as both Coraline x Other Mother - MCroft07
item.description: Trying out split paints, they're interesting and I think they look really cool. I couldn't make a decision for a while as to which version of Other Mother to paint, but I think it worked out pretty okay :) also this song is a bop Thanks to the people who are super kind and not inappropriate on my posts, it's appreciated. Faq: I use body paint and do these in a mirror It's from Coraline (a film) No, it is not AI. I painted my teeth with teeth paint. I use professional body paint covers I paint these by looking in a mirror, with brushes, and paint It takes between 6-10 hours each Body painting myself is my hobby/passion I do own clothes I did not go to art school, I am self taught If you want to see more I am MCroft07 on IG, twitter, Tiktok, YouTube I paint them live on twitch I like doing the lip syncs, they're fun for me while I paint them I can promise you there is not *someone else* painting them and I am pretending to paint myself. I am genuinely painting myself.
item.dc:creator: MCroft07

item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged XL Wednesday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
man that looks good
must never watch rasslin'
as is tradition
don't kink shame
German sexual harassment is more efficient
and the problem is?
seems about right
i don't know when dude works
mmm hmm
no
we've all been there
wait what
i have no idea what this means
hurry
these all offer incredible things
hello mr. pretzel
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!

item.title: Gaming Meme Phone Clearing Dump Pt. 4
item.description: Okay, I've typed some variation of this like 10 times now, so I'm just going to copy and paste this along with the links. The time has come for me to clear out some of the many memes I (for some reason) spent hours sorting into folders. Hope you all enjoy it. I'll post the links to the previous dumps as well for anyone who's interested. Star Wars Part 1: https://imgur.com/gallery/SVzbsKr Part 2: https://imgur.com/gallery/agPBjZY Part 3: https://imgur.com/gallery/3WbYPiO Part 4: https://imgur.com/gallery/D3xLtoh Part 5: https://imgur.com/gallery/hOagKbC Lord of the Rings https://imgur.com/gallery/YrdND58 Marvel https://imgur.com/gallery/J243dDD Harry Potter https://imgur.com/gallery/FCOoDNo Disney https://imgur.com/gallery/VFswkF4 Marvel https://imgur.com/gallery/tgBY85X Gaming Part 1: https://imgur.com/gallery/tgBY85X Part 2: https://imgur.com/gallery/dQN08Rs Part 3: https://imgur.com/gallery/uZJkc2E

item.title: Getting health care sucks here

item.title: Army Secretary fires 4-star General who meddled in promotion of unfit subordinate, first time in nearly 20 years
item.description: Army Secretary Christine Wormuth -- in a dramatic and rare move -- on Tuesday fired one of the service's top generals following an Army inspector general investigation that concluded he improperly intervened in the process for selecting senior commanders. Gen. Charles Hamilton, who had been the head of Army Materiel Command, was removed after what officials described as a flagrant abuse of authority aimed at securing a leadership role for a subordinate officer who was found unfit for command and had an inappropriate relationship with the general, according to the IG report. The move marks the first time in nearly 20 years an Army four-star general has been outright fired and comes after a Military.com investigation in March detailed how he attempted to intervene on behalf of the subordinate officer. The publication's report earlier this year immediately triggered both his suspension from the command leadership position and the inspector general probe. https://www.military.com/daily-news/2024/12/10/army-secretary-fires-4-star-general-who-meddled-promotion-of-unfit-0?ESRC=eb_241211.nl&utm_medium=email&utm_source=eb&utm_campaign=20241211
item.dc:creator: puzzledeagle
PiercedViking

item.title: Memes vol 447
item.description:
I sent this to my mom because she loved princess Di and she responded “Damn!”
“Whoever wins… we lose” no I don’t like that
As a Packers fan, this is amazing. It’s so clever
I’ve never had a pair of docs until a few months ago but I had an off brand pair that my dad called my “croc fartens”
Scruffy and Liza Jane tax
Nessie tax. Why is she sitting like this
Froggy tax
item.dc:creator: danat101

item.title: Yeah I do a bit of cooking as a hobby
item.description:
Here's just a ton of food I've made. If y'all want a recipe for something I'd be happy to put it in the comments. Above is one of the better platings I've done of Katsudon. It has become a comfort meal for me.
Before I owned a spring form pan I made some peach cobbler cheesecake using a cake pan with reinforced sides of aluminum. It worked surprisingly well, even though it was a pain to slice and serve. It was super decadent and over the top. I think this was for Christmas 2019
Japanese style curry has become another fall/winter favorite of mine to make from scratch. This one has chicken, carrots, potatoes, and onions.
My aunt has a lemon tree and gave me a ton of lemons one year so I made a lemon rosemary cake. The texture wasn't great but it was very tasty.
I made this pecan pie cheesecake for Thanksgiving a couple years ago without knowing my dad and my brother weren't huge fans of pecan pie or cheesecake, but that just meant there was more for my mom and myself.
Springform pan is way better for making cheesecake than a cake pan with aluminum foil.
I had a hankering for cinnamon rolls a while back so I tried making them. I was impressed they got so fluffy. I made a quick American buttercream that kind of melted because I was impatient and ate one shortly after pulling them out of the oven.
Where I used to live, I couldn't find any German food around me so one year I decided to make some German food roughly around Oktoberfest. This was my apple strudel fresh out of the oven.
And this was my apple strudel with a dusting of powdered sugar and a healthy dollop of whipped cream. I still think it was one of my greatest culinary triumphs.
My dad was jealous and wanted me to make German food for him as well, so when I visited for the holidays, I made everyone Rouladen with stewed root vegetables, spaetzle, and german red cabbage.
Frozen shrimp are super versatile so when you don't use all the shrimp to make shrimp and grits, it's easy to repurpose the rest for shrimp tacos with queso fresco, cabbage, radishes, pickled red onion, and avocado lime crema.
This was my first attempt at bibimbap. I did the beef under the broiler so it was a bit ugly, so I covered it with the sauce. The other things covering the rice were pickled carrots, brined cucumbers with spicy chili crisp, garlic spinach, and a fried egg.
Sometimes, I've been known to make a Dutch baby for breakfast. My favorite topping for them is peaches and maple syrup.
A couple years ago I got a molcajete for Christmas. This batch of guacamole was its inaugural use.
More German food. This time it is Kohlrouladen (stuffed cabbage) with gravy and boiled potatoes.
I started my current job last year just in time for the annual pie competition. I made my key lime pie that has gotten a lot of favorable reviews in the past and placed second. Don't worry, I did put whipped cream on top before serving it at the competition.
These pecan pie cookies are one of my favorites to make. These were for a work potluck at the end of last year.
I tried my hand at making butter chicken at the end of last year. It took me most of an afternoon, but it was well worth it. As a side I roasted some cauliflower with turmeric, coriander, and red chile powder.
Bread pudding is my favorite dessert, and I have been on a continuous hunt for ways to make it better. This apple bread pudding I made last Christmas was probably my best tasting one, but I'm still struggling with how to make it hold together better. Also on the right there is a bourbon caramel sauce because why not.
This was a cottage pie to use up the leftover prime rib that my dad cooked on his smoker for Christmas last year. it was a little liquidy, but very tasty.
I'm a big fan of grits and I'm frequently looking for new stuff to have with them. Here was my attempt at Grillades and grits which is a Cajun dish (usually for brunch or breakfast as I understand it) featuring pork and a wonderful gravy made from a dark roux.
This amalgam started off as Chorizo and grits but I kept adding things until I ended up with chorizo and bacon, black beans with green Chile, pico de Gallo, avocados, and radishes over cheese grits.
Pesto is one of my favorites to make, but I wanted to try something other than pasta this time, so I sauteed some chicken with a slice of mozz and topped it with pesto. Accompanied by some rice and roasted green beans.
I have had a recipe for gyoza saved on my phone for about 7 years, and this year I finally got around to it. I could not for the life of me find wanton wrappers to use so I made my own dough and rolled out 50 wrappers and froze this tray along with 12 more.
Then I steamed and pan fried them. Served with some rice, a hot sauce I got that seemed appropriate, and 50/50 soy sauce and rice vinegar with some chopped green onions.
I made some beef paprikash over egg noodles and in place of sour cream, we had Mexican crema so I used that. I was very happy with how it came out.
Shrimp and cheese grits. I make the shrimp with some sauteed holy Trinity and a pan sauce made from homemade shrimp stock.
This one I saved from Snapchat, thus the caption which explains it. My dad wanted Irish food so I made this while he was over.
This unexpectedly became one of my favorite dishes. I found a recipe on serious eats for Ganach Lupia which is an Armenian dish of braised green beans. I made it here with some beef short rib and it is incredible.
Served with rice pilaf and a healthy dollop of Greek yogurt as the recipe suggested.
For mother's day this year, I cooked some chicken provençal with fondant potatoes and asparagus. I had wanted to make fondant potatoes for quite a while and they turned out excellent. My only regret was being stingy with the sauce from the chicken.
More Korean food. This time it was a kimchi jjigae with seared tofu. I served it over rice, and my brother thought it was too spicy but I thought it was delicious. Just means that I got to eat all the leftovers.
Here I made Tefteli which are Russian meatballs in a tomato cream sauce, kinda similar to a pasta alla vodka sauce. Some of the meatballs kind of fell apart which I hope to fix next time I make it.
I didn't know Hawaiian barbeque was a thing until one day at my first job out of college a Hawaiian food truck came and parked outside. I fell in love with kalua pork, macaroni salad, and spam musubi, but this was my first attempt at making it. The macaroni salad was spot on, and the pork tasted great. The only issue was the cabbage got a bit overdone because I did it in my slow cooker.
This is Broa, Portuguese corn bread. It's a crusty yeasted bread that is great for soaking up soups.
I made it to go with a stew of chouriço, chicken, scallops, potatoes, onions, carrots, red peppers, and spinach. I made it for my parents when they visited last week and they loved it.
When I made the Hawaiian barbeque in my slow cooker, I saved the broth to make some really good ramen. I clearly didn't put enough ramen down to make a bed for everything, but I pickled some thinly sliced ginger (reminder that mandolins are SHARP), sauteed some spinach and bacon (in place of pork belly), and soft boiled some eggs and let them soak up some soy sauce
We got a bunch of apples from an orchard so one of the things I made to get rid of them was Poullet a la Normandie (Norman chicken). I served it with some mashed potatoes and roasted parsnips to make everything as monochromatic as possible. (Please ignore the ads we get in the mail)
Have you ever put hollandaise on an omelette? If not you should. It's awesome.
The reason I made hollandaise was to use up a baguette and some bacon.
I made my parents paella with shrimp, chicken, and chorizo for their anniversary this year and was too slow to snap a picture before it was mostly gone. The Dutch oven wasn't able to get the nice crust that I was expecting but other than that it turned out great.
This year's offering for the pie competition was a pear pie with a crumble topping. It won this year likely because there were significantly fewer people that brought pies because we had a lot of folks on travel for work.
I had been hankering for something with leeks in it, so I made some leek risotto with seared scallops in a brown butter lemon garlic pan sauce. It was so rich and delicious. My mom who loves scallops said I cooked them perfectly.
For Thanksgiving this year we decided to do a big brunch and an early afternoon dinner, so for brunch I made biscuits with green Chile gravy and a pear galette. The biscuits and gravy were not particularly photogenic but super tasty.
item.dc:creator: isyourTVrunning
